description: A RollingUpdate runs old and new versions against an incompatible database schema, corrupting data during overlap.
body: Failure mode: a backward-incompatible change ships as a RollingUpdate, so during the overlap window old pods write the old schema while new pods expect the new one, producing read errors, dropped writes, or corruption. Detection signals: errors only during the rollout window; data written by one version unreadable by the other; failures clear once all pods are on one version. Do not roll out schema-incompatible changes with simple RollingUpdate. Safer fallback: use an expand/contract migration so both versions tolerate the schema, deploy in ordered phases, and verify mixed-version traffic produces no read/write errors before contracting.
